hook.scenario: Swiggy promises 10-minute delivery. The rider covers 3 km. Half the route is traffic (10 km/hr), half is open road. Can they make it? TSD is about breaking down these real-world constraints.

mental_model.intuition: Distance is the output. Speed is the rate. Time is the duration. (D = S * T)
mental_model.description: They are perfectly locked together. If distance is constant, speed and time are inversely proportional (double the speed, half the time).

formula.explain: And its derivatives: Speed = Distance / Time, Time = Distance / Speed. Ensure all units match (km/hr with hours, m/s with seconds) before multiplying!

shortcuts.title: km/hr to m/s Conversion
shortcuts.trick: Multiply by 5/18 to go km/hr -> m/s. Multiply by 18/5 to go m/s -> km/hr.

shortcuts.title: Average Speed for Equal Distances
shortcuts.trick: If going at speed A and returning at speed B over the same distance, Avg Speed = 2AB / (A + B).
shortcuts.example: Go at 40 km/hr, return at 60 km/hr. Avg = (2 * 40 * 60) / 100 = 48 km/hr. (NOT 50!)

common_mistakes.title: Simple Average of Speeds
common_mistakes.explanation: Averaging speeds (A+B)/2 only works if they traveled for equal TIME, not equal DISTANCE.

common_mistakes.title: Unit Mismatch
common_mistakes.explanation: Multiplying km/hr by minutes without converting minutes to hours first.

recall_questions.q: If speed is doubled for a fixed distance, what happens to the time taken?
What is the average speed formula for equal distances traveled at speeds x and y?

oa_questions.question: A person walks from his house at 4 km/hr and reaches office 5 minutes late. If his speed is 5 km/hr, he reaches 10 minutes early. What is the distance to the office?
oa_questions.approach: Difference in time = 15 mins = 1/4 hr. Let distance be D. D/4 - D/5 = 1/4. D/20 = 1/4 => D = 5 km.

oa_questions.question: A man covers half his journey at 6 km/hr and the remaining half at 3 km/hr. What is his average speed?
